---
title: "Hi! i'm grace."
output:
  html_document:
    theme: readable
    css: custom.css
---

*Keywords:* *Human factors in transportation*, *Road safety*, *Mixed-traffic driving environments*, *Human-automation interaction*, *Driving behavior*, *Pedestrian path-planning*, *Statistical modeling*, *Human subjects research*

> I am a human subjects researcher. I am interested in building human-like models to describe road user behavior in shared urban spaces. Specifically, my dissertation work explores cross-cultural representations of vulnerable road users in mixed-traffic driving environments, aiming to develop *fair*, *safe*, and *shareable* analytical representations of real-time negotiation between motorized and nonmotorized road users.

### tldr;

-   [.research](/research/projects.html) page for more details past and present work.
-   [.skills](/research/skills.html) page for details on my software, hardware, and technical experience.
-   [.FUN](/research/skills.html) page for AFK nonsense.

### Current events

I'm a Ph.D. Candidate studying Transportation Systems in Civil & Urban Engineering at New York University's Tandon School of Engineering in Downtown Brooklyn, NY. Some current project work:

__Pedestrian exposure for crash prediction.__ Prediction models are only as good as the data they learn from.

-   *How does the inclusion of pedestrian activity in the microenvironment improve the performance of intersection-level crash rate prediction?*
-   *How do we develop a framework for identifying, analyzing, and learning from prediction model misclassifications?*

__Unregulated pedestrian behavior in mixed-traffic driving environments.__ Locale-specific feature learning is necessary training to ensure safe deployment around vulnerable road users. This project is jointly conducted at NYU and THI (Germany) to examine inter-cultural differences of pedestrian adherence to road regulation.

-   *Are different locales identifiable by their vulnerable road user behavior?*

### Contact

[*Water cooler coffee?* <sup>1,2</sup>]{style="font-size: 120%;"} I'm best over a cup of joe. Please reach out to collaborate or discuss my research!

[grace.douglas\@nyu.edu](mailto:grace.douglas@nyu.edu)

[LinkedIn](https://www.linkedin.com/in/grace-douglas-a92a44132/)

::: row
::: {.col-md-4 .img-container}
_AFK_ grace ![](images/selfie-window.jpeg){.img-fluid .rounded}
:::

::: {.col-md-4 .img-container}
is learning to boulder ... ![](images/boulder.jpeg){.img-fluid .rounded}
:::

::: {.col-md-4 .img-container}
but cannot wait for shavansana. ![](images/yoga.jpg){.img-fluid .rounded}
:::
:::

<small> [1] Li, X., & Fussell, S. R. (2023, July). Negotiating Water Cooler Conversations Remotely: Perspectives from PhD Students During COVID-19. In International Conference on Human-Computer Interaction (pp. 142-154). Cham: Springer Nature Switzerland.

[2] Koch, T., & Denner, N. (2022). Informal communication in organizations: work time wasted at the water-cooler or crucial exchange among co-workers?. Corporate Communications: An International Journal, 27(3), 494-508. </small>
